title: Software-mageddon: Agentic AI Wipes Out Billions in Outsourcing Stocks
content:
The rapid deployment of autonomous agentic AI tools has triggered a seismic shift in the global technology sector, recently wiping out approximately $800 billion in stock value in a single week. This event, dubbed "software-mageddon," hit India's outsourcing industry particularly hard, causing major market indices to plummet. Traditional outsourcing companies, which have long relied on a billable-hours model, are now being forced to radically rethink their business strategies. AI-powered coding tools are proving to be more accessible and capable of delivering extremely speedy results. Consequently, industry leaders are urgently exploring how to reskill their massive workforces to remain relevant in this new era.

On sprawling corporate campuses in cities like Bengaluru, the day-to-day routine of employees remains seemingly normal, but the underlying nature of their work is changing drastically. Tasks that once took teams several months to plan, design, code, and test are now being completed in a fraction of the time. Advanced models from companies like OpenAI and Anthropic are automating routine programming, allowing human engineers to focus on higher-level strategy and AI governance. New recruits are now trained in dozens of modern technology stacks, emphasizing agentic AI and data engineering over traditional legacy systems. Ultimately, surviving this technological transition will require companies to adapt rapidly rather than simply relying on past strengths.
